name: Old-Fashioned Pot Roast
recipeInstructions:
1. Sprinkle the roast with 1 tablespoon flour.
2. In a Dutch oven, brown the roast on all sides in half of the butter.
3. Add the water, bouillon, onion, celery, salt, and pepper; bring to a boil.
4. Reduce heat; cover and simmer for 1 hour.
5. Add carrots; cover and simmer 45-60 minutes longer or until meat is tender.
6. Remove meat and carrots to a serving platter and keep warm.
7. Strain cooking juices; set aside.
8. In the same Dutch oven, melt remaining butter.
9. Stir in remaining flour; cook and stir until bubbly.
10. Add 2 cups of the cooking juices and blend until smooth.
11. Cook and stir until thickened; add additional cooking juices until gravy has desired consistency.
